suggestions.action: Add indexes on filter/join columns to avoid full table scans

executive_summary: Query scans 1 table; returns 3,000 rows in 0.44 ms. Main finding: full scan of users (3,000 rows).

query.raw: /* select#1 */ select `testdb`.`users`.`id` AS `id`,`testdb`.`users`.`name` AS `name`,`testdb`.`users`.`email` AS `email`,`testdb`.`users`.`country` AS `country`,`testdb`.`users`.`created_at` AS `created_at` from `testdb`.`users`
query.beautified:
select testdb.users.id AS id,testdb.users.name AS name,testdb.users.email AS email,testdb.users.country AS country,testdb.users.created_at AS created_at
from testdb.users

teach_hooks.query_sql: /* select#1 */ select `testdb`.`users`.`id` AS `id`,`testdb`.`users`.`name` AS `name`,`testdb`.`users`.`email` AS `email`,`testdb`.`users`.`country` AS `country`,`testdb`.`users`.`created_at` AS `created_at` from `testdb`.`users`

complexity.rationale: Full table scan: the storage engine returns every row; cost scales with the table size.
